webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Each click triggers a POST to the URL you register. Slack and Discord incoming webhooks are auto-detected and converted to their native message format; everything else gets the kurl JSON payload with an HMAC-signed X-Kurl-Signature header. Up to 5 per link.
webhooks.issuedHint: This secret is shown only once. Use it on the receiver to verify the X-Kurl-Signature (sha256=hex) header.
webhooks.issuedFormatDiscord: Discord URL detected — clicks are sent as Discord embeds (no signature verification needed).
webhooks.issuedFormatSlack: Slack URL detected — clicks are sent as Slack messages (no signature verification needed).
webhooks.optBatch: Batch mode (one POST every 5s, max 50 events)

destinations.title: A/B split / geo-targeting
destinations.description: Distribute clicks across destinations by weight, or send visitors from specific countries to a tailored page. Country-tagged variants only fire for matching visitors; otherwise traffic falls back to country-agnostic variants, then the original URL. Up to 4 per link.

routeMetrics.title: Per-route traffic & latency
routeMetrics.desc: Requests, p95/p99 (ms), and 5xx rate grouped by URI/method. Pick a window for recent slices (backed by an in-memory minute-by-minute ring) or 'all' for the cumulative view since process start. Click ▶ on a row to see its status code distribution.

linkMetrics.title: Per-link performance
linkMetrics.desc: Redirect latency (p50/p95/p99), in-window click count, and error rate per short_code. The in-memory ring tracks up to the top 500 recently active codes. Click ▶ on a row to see its outcome breakdown (redirect / not_found / expired / blocked / ...).
linkMetrics.empty: No redirects captured for any link in this window

requestMetrics.title: Request log (raw)
requestMetrics.desc: Backed by the new request_metrics table — every finished HTTP request is captured asynchronously, so this section can answer per-code outcome questions and drill into individual requests, things the timer-backed sections above cannot. Use 'Show sample' to preview the layout when the table is empty (fresh deploy / pre-launch).

about.title: kurl — URL shortener with click analytics
about.lead: Shrink long URLs and see where your clicks come from. Free, no ads.
about.section1Body: Most shorteners hide analytics behind a paywall. kurl gives you device, OS, browser, channel, country, and hour-of-day breakdowns for free.
about.section2Body: Each visit is checked for bots and malicious URLs, then redirected. Click data is stored in a wide, query-friendly schema so charts load fast even at scale.
about.section3Body: Spring Boot · MySQL · Redis · Next.js 14 · AWS ECS Fargate. Source on GitHub.

learn.metaTitle: Short links · URL shortener · Link in bio guide · kurl
learn.metaDescription: What URL shortening is, how short links differ from link-in-bio pages, and how kurl combines both — answered in one place.
learn.title: Short links and link-in-bio, explained for beginners
learn.lead: What URL shortening is, what kurl does, how a profile page differs from a single short link — the questions people search for, in one page.

q1.a:
URL shortening replaces a long address (e.g. https://example.com/very/long/path?utm_source=...) with a short one (e.g. kurl.me/abc1234). Short link and shortened URL mean the same thing.

It's useful where character limits matter (social posts), where people type the URL by hand (business cards, posters), and as an attribution layer — most shortener services track clicks so you can see which channels actually drive traffic.

q2.q: How is a short link different from a link-in-bio page?
q2.a:
A short link is a 1:1 mapping: one long URL → one short URL. Best for a single destination — Twitter post, Instagram comment, business card.

A link-in-bio page collects many links behind one URL. Since Instagram lets you put only one link in your profile, creators use kurl.me/yourname as that one link, pointing visitors to a mini page with their email, booking, store, blog, and socials.

kurl does both — shorten individual URLs, and build a profile page that bundles them.

q3.a:
kurl is a free URL shortener + link-in-bio service. No ads, no required signup to shorten, and free analytics: device, country, channel, time-of-day.

Sign in with Google in one click and your shortened links persist, plus you can build a profile page at kurl.me/yourname in five minutes.

q4.q: What can a profile page contain?
q4.a:
Eight block types: photo + bio + contacts (phone/email/address) + socials + events + galleries + product cards + calendar embeds. Mix and match per persona.

A cafe owner might show: map + hours + Instagram/reservation links + menu gallery. An instructor: workshop events + 1:1 booking link + portfolio. A DJ: upcoming gigs + Spotify Artist link + photos. All on one page.

q5.q: How deep do the analytics go?
q5.a:
Per short link: total / human vs bot / unique visitors / daily + hourly charts / day-of-week × hour heatmap / country + city / device / browser / OS / referrer hosts / UTM campaigns. Same depth as Bitly Pro, but free.

Profile pages get a parallel /profile/stats dashboard for visit counts and the same demographic breakdowns.

q6.a:
All core features are free — shortening, analytics, profile pages, all eight block types.

The Pro plan adds custom domains with auto-TLS, unlimited links, and indefinite stats retention. See the pricing page for details.

meta.title: Pricing · Free URL shortener, Pro for unlimited - kurl
meta.description: Start free with URL shortening and click analytics. Pro adds unlimited links, custom-domain auto TLS, and permanent stats retention.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.p1: These terms govern your use of kurl. Using the service means you accept them.
terms.p2: kurl is for shortening lawful URLs only. Shortening is rejected or revoked for: malicious/phishing destinations, spam, copyright violations, hate content, doxxing.
terms.p3: Public http/https URLs only. Private IPs, localhost, file:// and similar are blocked.
terms.p4: We screen with Google Safe Browsing plus custom bot heuristics. Abusive accounts may be suspended without notice.
terms.p5: Service provided AS-IS without availability guarantees. Operators are not liable for data loss or downtime.
terms.p6: Terms may change with notice via the "Last updated" timestamp and, where possible, email.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.p1: kurl collects the minimum data needed and anonymizes by default. This policy follows GDPR Article 13 and Korea's PIPA.
privacy.p2: OAuth sign-in: email, provider (Google), signup date. Shortening: original URL, short code, expiry. Clicks: timestamp, User-Agent, Referer, IP with last octet masked, GeoIP-derived country/region/city.
privacy.p3: Service operation (redirect, stats), abuse protection, security incident response. Never sold or used for marketing.
privacy.p4: Anonymous links auto-expire after 24h. Authenticated links persist until you delete. Expired links + their clicks are auto-cleaned 30 days after expiry. Account deletion erases everything immediately.
privacy.p5: Stored IPs are masked (IPv4 last octet / IPv6 beyond first two groups). Raw IPs exist transiently in memory and logs only — never written to the database.
privacy.p6: GDPR Article 17 (erasure) and Article 20 (portability) honored. Settings page exposes data download (JSON) and permanent account deletion.
privacy.p7: Google OAuth (email, name), Google Safe Browsing API (URL hashes for malware checks), MaxMind GeoLite2 (offline GeoIP — no outbound calls).
privacy.p8: One HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Strict cookie for the JWT refresh token. No analytics or ad cookies.
privacy.p9: Data inquiries: GitHub issues or the operator's email.

profile.addEventDescription: A workshop, popup, or deadline. Visitors can add it to their own calendar in one tap.
profile.addEmailFormDescription: A short form visitors fill to leave their email. Collected addresses appear on your dashboard / CSV export.
profile.emailFormFieldSubtitlePlaceholder: e.g. New product launches, class openings, limited menus — you'll hear it first.
profile.emailFormFieldSubtitleHint: One or two sentences telling visitors why they should leave their email.
profile.emailFormHowItWorks: Emails visitors leave land in your private 'leads' dashboard. Export to CSV and import into Mailchimp / your email tool.
profile.leadsDisabledTitle: Add an email form to collect leads here

stats.title: My profile visit stats
stats.intro: Who's reading your /u/username page, and from where. Same depth as your short-link stats.

heatmap.title: Day × hour heatmap
heatmap.desc: When visitors land on your page

utmCampaigns.title: Campaigns (utm_campaign)
utmCampaigns.desc: From the campaign builder or external sends

stats.publicIntro: Aggregated stats the profile owner chose to share publicly.

publicNotAvailable.title: Stats aren't public
publicNotAvailable.body: This profile owner has kept their visit stats private.

visibility.title: Show visit stats publicly
visibility.publicHint: Anyone can view aggregated stats at /u/{username}/stats. Individual visitor data is never exposed.
visibility.privateHint: Only you can see these for now. Turn on to let visitors view your charts.

leads.title: Collected emails
leads.csvExcludesOptedOut: Entries marked as opted-out are automatically excluded from the CSV.
leads.errorHint: Please try again in a moment. If it keeps failing, try refreshing the page.
leads.emptyHint: Add an Email signup block to your profile and submissions will show up here.

campaign.title: Campaign message builder
campaign.intro: Every link in your body is auto-shortened and tagged with UTM params. Paste the result into Gmail / Mailchimp and your clicks will roll up per campaign.
campaign.bodyPlaceholder:
Hi!

Excited to share our latest launch.
Details: https://example.com/launch

Thanks!
campaign.bodyNoLinks: Include any http(s) link and it will be auto-shortened.
campaign.buildFailed: Couldn't build the message. Try again in a moment.
campaign.outputHint: Copy the body below and paste it into your sending tool.
campaign.sendHint: Send from your own tool. Clicks are filterable by utm_campaign on the link dashboard.

profile.addContactCardDescription: Fields shown on your public page. Visitors can download a .vcf to save your contact directly.
profile.contactFieldLogoHint: Company / personal logo — displayed as a square on the card.
profile.contactFieldLogoFocalHint: Drag the image to pick which part fills the square.
profile.addGalleryDescription: Image URLs to display as a swipeable carousel. Up to {max} images.
profile.addGalleryDescriptionUpload: Up to {max} — visitors swipe through. First image is the cover.
profile.galleryReorderHint: Drag to reorder · the first photo is the cover visitors land on
profile.addTextDescription: Section blurb, announcement, or short paragraph. Supports markdown (headings, bold, lists, links).
profile.textFieldPlaceholder:
e.g. ## Updates
New menu items! [Read more](https://...)
profile.textFieldIconHint: Shown top-left of the highlight box — e.g. 💡 / 📢 / ⚠️ / 🎉
profile.addEmbedDescription: Paste a YouTube, Vimeo, Spotify, or SoundCloud URL. It'll render as a player on your public page.

settings.timezoneHint: Used as the report time zone on stats pages.
settings.languageHint: You can also change this from the language switcher in the header.
settings.exportHint: User info + all links + click events (IPs are masked).
settings.deleteHint: Your account enters a 30-day grace period after deletion. Sign back in within that window to restore everything; after 30 days the account and all links/stats are permanently removed. Anonymous links are not affected.
settings.deleteConfirmDesc: Sign back in within 30 days to restore. After that this is permanent. Type "DELETE" to confirm.

twofa.title: Two-factor authentication (2FA)
twofa.description: Add a TOTP authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, etc.) for an extra step at sign-in. Save the 10 recovery codes shown right after enabling — they're your backup if you lose access to the app.
twofa.scanTitle: Scan the QR with your authenticator
twofa.scanHint: Or type the manual key into your app if scanning isn't possible.
twofa.confirmRegenerateHint: Enter a current code to issue 10 fresh recovery codes. Existing codes will stop working.
twofa.confirmDisableHint: Enter a current code or recovery code to turn 2FA off.
twofa.recoveryTitle: Save your recovery codes somewhere safe
twofa.recoveryHint: Each code works only once. Use them if you lose access to your authenticator app — print them or store in a password manager.

apiKeys.title: API keys
apiKeys.description: Use these from scripts or automations that call the kurl API. The full key is shown once — copy it somewhere safe immediately.
apiKeys.justIssuedHint: You won't be able to see this again. Store it like a password.
apiKeys.revokeConfirm: Revoking this key disables it immediately. Continue?

customDomains.title: Custom domains
customDomains.description: Serve short links from your own domain (e.g. go.brand.com). Add TXT + CNAME records — we auto-verify in the background (polls DNS for up to 10 min). Easiest TLS path: front your domain with Cloudflare free plan.
customDomains.empty: No domains yet. Enter your domain above to start.
customDomains.verifyFailed: TXT record not found. DNS may take 1–5 min to propagate.
customDomains.deleteConfirm: Remove this domain? Short URLs on this domain will stop working immediately.
customDomains.dnsTxtHint: Ownership check — add this TXT record at your DNS provider
customDomains.dnsCnameHint: Traffic routing — add this CNAME record at the same provider

entry.title: QR campaigns
entry.description: Split posters and flyers into batches, then see which batch performed best. A separate mode from regular short links.

batchDialogs.editDescription: You can edit only the name, distributor, area, quantity, and memo. The short URL printed in the QR stays unchanged.
batchDialogs.name: Batch name
batchDialogs.deleteWarningTitle: The short URL will be deleted too.
batchDialogs.deleteWarningBody: People scanning already-printed QR codes will see a link-not-found page. This is safe before ordering print, but after distribution you should usually keep it alive with an end policy.

new.title: New QR campaign
new.intro: Choose the active period and end policy, then add distribution batches inside it.
new.startHint: Usually start now. Schedule it only when print or ordering dates require it.
new.endHint: Printed QR codes follow this policy after the campaign ends.
new.defaultUrlHint: Leave blank if each batch should use its own URL.

policy.keepBody: Printed QR codes keep sending visitors to the original URLs after the campaign ends.
policy.redirectBody: Send printed QRs to a review page, next campaign, waitlist, or any follow-up URL.

new.messagePlaceholder:
e.g. This campaign has ended. See you at the next event in December.
Contact — @kurl_official
new.messageHint: Blank uses the default message. Line breaks are preserved ·
new.redirectHint: The redirect URL is applied to printed QR codes when the campaign ends.

detail.confirmEnd:
End this QR campaign now?
The end policy applies immediately to printed QRs.
detail.confirmArchive:
Archive this QR campaign?
It will be hidden from the list, but data stays intact.

prepare.title: Assets for print shops and Canva
prepare.description: QR codes and metadata for {count} batches. Drop them into posters or flyers before ordering print.

policy.savedEnded: Message saved. Use reapply end policy to push it to printed QRs.
policy.messagePlaceholder: e.g. This campaign has ended. See you at the next event in December.
policy.defaultMessageHint: Leave the message blank to show the default notice.

batches.title: Distribution batches
batches.description: One unit of printed distribution using the same QR. Split by distributor or area for meaningful analysis.
batches.emptyDescription: Add the first batch to issue a QR code. Scan it before ordering print.

batchesNew.pasteEmpty: No rows found in the pasted text
batchesNew.title: Add distribution batches
batchesNew.defaultUrlActive: Default destination URL is set. Leave the destination field blank to use . Enter a URL only for batches that need a different destination.
batchesNew.defaultUrlMissing: No default destination URL is set. Each batch needs its own destination URL.
batchesNew.pasteColumns: Column order: Batch name / Distributor / Area / Quantity / Destination URL / Memo (tab or comma separated)
batchesNew.pastePlaceholder:
Shinjuku Station East	A	Shinjuku East	500	
Okubo	B	Okubo	300	https://example.com/okubo
batchesNew.invalidRows: {count} rows have input errors. Fix the highlighted cells.

posterBuilder.pdfOnly: Only PDF files can be uploaded
posterBuilder.pdfReadFailed: Could not read the PDF. The file may be damaged.
posterBuilder.composed: Created a merged PDF with {count} pages
posterBuilder.title: Add QR to poster
posterBuilder.introCampaign: <name></name> · Duplicate the PDF once per batch ({count} total), each with its own QR, then download one merged PDF.
posterBuilder.toolHint: Finish the design in Canva, InDesign, PowerPoint, or another tool, export a PDF, then place only the QR here. This is a coordinate compositor, not a design tool.
posterBuilder.noBatchesHint: Add distribution batches first. (QR campaign detail → Add batch)
posterBuilder.notSavedHint: QR placement is not saved. If you return with the same PDF later, redraw the box. Campaign print runs are treated as one-off orders.
posterBuilder.dropTitle: Drop a PDF here or click to upload
posterBuilder.dragHint: Drag the QR box to move it, or resize it from the bottom-right handle.
posterBuilder.previewFirstBatch: Preview uses the first batch (1 of {count}). Each batch gets a different QR.
posterBuilder.previewPlaceholder: No batches yet, so the preview uses a placeholder QR. Real composition is available after adding batches.

printSheet.title: A4 QR sheet
printSheet.introCampaign: <name></name> · Save as PDF through browser print (Cmd/Ctrl + P).
printSheet.empty: No batches to print. Add a batch first.

campaignStats.title: Stats
campaignStats.introCampaign: <name></name> · Counts clicks after campaign start, excluding bots and previews.

groups.distributorHint: Clicks per 100 sheets — comparable even when distributors handled different quantities.
groups.areaHint: Use this to decide the next distribution location.

compare.title: Compare with another campaign
compare.description: Compare first vs second runs, or different seasonal campaigns.
compare.selectHint: Choose a campaign from the dropdown above.

recommendation.title: Next distribution recommendation
recommendation.description: Redistribute the same total of {total} sheets. Baseline average is {average} per 100.
recommendation.footnote: Recommendation based on efficiency ratios. Under 30% of average is pruned; the rest is reallocated by ratio, capped at 3x per batch.

testScans.title: Pre-start test scans
testScans.description: {count} before campaign start. Excluded from analytics totals.

byBatch.empty: No distribution batches yet. Batch performance appears here after you add one.
byBatch.title: Performance by batch
byBatch.description: Sorted by clicks descending. Bars are relative to the max row.

homeFaq.title: Frequently asked questions
homeFaq.a1: Anonymous links auto-expire after 24h. Links you make signed in last until you delete them.
homeFaq.q2: Can I use it without signing in?
homeFaq.a2: Yes — shortening works without an account. Stats and permanent storage need Google sign-in.
homeFaq.a3: On the My Links page click any short code to open the 8-dimension stats page.
homeFaq.q4: Will previews show in KakaoTalk / Slack?
homeFaq.a4: Yes — preview cards work on KakaoTalk, Slack, and Discord. When those apps fetch the link to build a card, kurl returns a preview page with title, description, and image instead of redirecting right away.
homeFaq.a5: We screen with Google Safe Browsing at shorten time. Flagged URLs are rejected.
homeFaq.a6: Yes — kurl offers a developer API. Get a key under Settings > API keys and call the shorten / stats endpoints from your own code.

campaignsApp.authDesc: QR campaigns are available to signed-in users only.
campaignsApp.title: QR Campaigns
campaignsApp.subtitleLoading: Operational units for offline QR distribution

onboarding.title: Improve offline promotion like online ads
onboarding.body: Split posters, flyers, and QR codes by distributor, area, and batch. Measure which batch performed best, then tune the next distribution with data.

distribution.title: Optimize distribution batches
distribution.body: Area A: 500 flyers → 80 visitors. Area B: 500 → 5. Increase A next time and reduce B.

distributor.title: Compare distributor performance
distributor.body: Distributor A: 1,000 flyers → 120 visits. B: 1,000 → 18. Useful for settlement, validation, and indirect proof of distribution.

copy.title: A/B test copy and design
copy.body: "Free consultation" vs "50% off first month" — run two versions in the same area and see which earns clicks.

location.title: Plan by location
location.body: Station exit responds well, residential street does not — choose the next poster or banner spot accordingly.

reuse.title: Reuse QR after the campaign
reuse.body: Event ended but posters remain? Route expired QR codes to reviews, a waitlist, or the next promotion.

conversion.title: Track beyond clicks
conversion.body: Measure inquiries, bookings, coupon downloads, and purchases so you know which batch created real customers.

onboarding.createHint: For the first campaign, you only need a name and an end time.

qrDownload.zipDescription: All batch QR codes in one package, ready to hand off to a printer or designer.
qrDownload.singleDescription: QR PNG for one distribution batch.
qrDownload.ecHint: Use a higher level for outdoor or dirty environments.
qrDownload.labelHint: Print the batch name below the QR so printers can identify it.
qrDownload.notSaved: Download options are not saved. You can download the same campaign multiple times with different options.

meta.title: QR Campaigns | Flyer & Poster QR Performance — kurl
meta.description: Track flyer, poster, coupon QRs split by area, vendor, or batch. Use clicks-per-100 to choose your next drop zone.

hero.title2: instantly see which ones got scanned.
hero.sub: Auto-generate a different QR per area, exit, or distributor. Use clicks per 100 flyers to pick your next drop zone.

s1.line2: But where did they actually work?

s2.aux: Batch = a group of flyers sharing the same QR.

s3.aux: Bring a Canva / InDesign PDF. Mark where the QR goes.

s4.line2: Double down on Shibuya next round.
s4.aux: Where to drop next — the clicks tell you.

s5.aux: Use cases — compare with the same method.

cta.title: Where to drop next — the clicks tell you.
cta.note: Your short links and stats share the same account.

mock.posterStep3: Download merged PDF — one page per batch
mock.timelineFoot: Same QR, the linked page switches.

workspaceGate.title: Sign in to start writing
workspaceGate.body: Your posts, drafts, and series live here. Sign in to pick up where you left off.

blogLogin.title: Sign in to write
blogLogin.subtitle: Your posts, drafts, and series live here. Sign in to pick up where you left off.

postAnalytics.title: Analytics
postAnalytics.description: Per-post view counter (v0). Precise unique visitors / read time / scroll depth come after L3 tracking JS.

ctaLibrary.description: Manage the CTA buttons you embed in posts in one place. Reuse the same CTA across multiple posts.

abuseReports.title: Abuse Reports
abuseReports.subtitle: Abuse report intake list. CSAM auto-quarantine + DMCA are on a separate track.
abuseReports.takedownConfirm: Unpublish this post? It leaves the public feed (the author keeps the draft).
